page: Microsoft Azure F16 is an F-family virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, equipped with a 50 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 262 GB, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, reflected in the stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory read bandwidth is average at 59.03 GB/sec for large blocks, but large-block write bandwidth is weak at 24.29 GB/sec. The server handles tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations at average performance tiers.

The F16 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, leveraging its dedicated physical cores. The primary tradeoff is the older Intel Xeon v4 architecture, which limits single-threaded execution speed and lacks newer instruction sets. This server is best aligned with multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F16 is an F-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz with 32.0 GB of RAM and 262 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a compute-optimized 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmarks show weak single-core performance but average multi-core scaling, with a Passmark CPU score of 16171.74 and average software compilation speeds. Memory read bandwidth is average, while large-block write bandwidth is weak. Qualitatively, the F16 offers a cost-efficient option for dedicated compute resources where high memory density is unnecessary. It is suitable for multi-threaded workloads, including static web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of bundled local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for resource-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 physical cores at 2.3 GHz
32.0 GB system memory and 262 GB bundled local storage
Average multi-core scaling and software compilation performance
Weak single-core execution and large-block memory write speeds
Appropriate for static web serving, database operations, and compilation
